name: Glen Canyon Park

summary_en: Archive documents describe Glen Canyon Park as a large natural canyon — a 2011 draft environmental impact report calls it a 70-acre natural and recreational area, while 1961-1962 Diamond Heights redevelopment reports describe an 80-acre wooded canyon with a day camp adjoining the neighborhood. Later records track its upkeep: the 2008 Clean & Safe Neighborhood Parks bond and a 2012 certificate of exemption cover renovation of the children's play area and day-camp facility, and Board of Supervisors and Arts Commission minutes from 1995 onward address the recreation center and park traffic. The park is operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line is about $250M. No contract in the data names the park.
